rulings.Case Title: DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC. VS. ROBERT POYNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Robert And Nili Poynter'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of A Referee To Examine Account (W/Crt Rpt Fee)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2022 line 1. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ROBERT POYNTER AND NILI POYNTER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE TO EXAMINE ACCOUNT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for appointment of referee is GRANTED as follows: 1) Parties are to meet and confer regarding a referee and submit a joint proposed order to the Court no later than January 17, 2022. The order shall also specify the deadline for referee's report under CCP 643 and for any motions challenging such report; OR 2) If parties cannot agree on a referee, each party (parties shall meet and confer if submissions will be per side or per party) shall submit a list of 3 proposed referees, ready and willing to serve no later than January 17, 2022. Each party may object to one proposed referee no later than January 21, 2022. Defendants shall prepare an order appointing a referee leaving the name of the referee blank. The order shall also specify the deadline for referee's report under CCP 643 and for any motions challenging such report. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN C.H. YUE VS. MIKE NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 3, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT MIKE NGUYEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant argues that Plaintiff did not meet the requirements of Rent Ordinance Section 37.9(c) because the Landlord failed to properly state the grounds for the eviction in the Landlord's Declaration attached to the Sixty Day Notice of Termination of Tenancy. Defendant argues that Plaintiff did not provide notice as to whether the Landlord was seeking possession for the Landlord's own use or for the Landlord's relatives use. However, in paragraph 1 of his Declaration, the Landlord indicates he seeks possession in order to move into the unit himself. Therefore, the evidence does not support Defendant's argument, and the Defendant has failed to show that Defendant is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law regarding whether Plaintiff strictly complied with the relevant ordinance. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
